title: Half of Aging Men Are Losing a Chromosome. It May Be Killing Them.
description: A growing fraction of men's cells silently discard the Y chromosome as they age — and researchers have linked this loss to Alzheimer's disease, multiple cancers, heart failure, and early death. The Y chromosome was dismissed as genetic junk for decades. New evidence suggests it may be one of the most important aging clocks in the male body — and that smoking and metabolic stress are accelerating its disappearance.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Half of Aging Men Are Losing a Chromosome. It May Be Killing Them.
